findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Re-verify that `_UUID_CREATE`, `_UUID_A`, `_UUID_B`, `_UUID_C` in `test_reducer_tag_delta.py` are genuinely unreferenced before scoping their removal — a grep shows they are actively used as `_write_event` uuid arguments (lines 63, 71, 81, 92–93, etc.) and must NOT be removed.
evidence: tests/scripts/reducer/test_reducer_tag_delta.py:63: _write_event(ticket_dir, 1000, _UUID_CREATE, 'CREATE', data)
tests/scripts/reducer/test_reducer_tag_delta.py:71: _write_event(d, 2000, _UUID_A, 'TAG_DELTA', ...)
tests/scripts/reducer/test_reducer_tag_delta.py:93: _write_event(d, 3000, _UUID_B, 'TAG_DELTA', ...)
Multiple further uses of _UUID_CREATE/_UUID_A/_UUID_B/_UUID_C throughout the file as positional uuid arguments to _write_event
Plan asserts these constants are 'dead' and scopes their removal
findings.finding: The plan asserts that `_UUID_CREATE`, `_UUID_A`, `_UUID_B`, `_UUID_C` in `tests/scripts/reducer/test_reducer_tag_delta.py` are dead fixture constants safe to remove. In fact, all four are actively used as event-UUID arguments in `_write_event` calls throughout that file (lines 63, 71, 81, 92, 93, 107, 108, 122, 123, 136, 137, 148, 149, 160, 171, 182, 208). Removing them would cause immediate NameError failures across every test in the file.
findings.location: Tier 2 bullet: 'dead test-fixture constants (_UUID_*/_ALIAS_*/_ENGINE_DIR) in … test_reducer_tag_delta'
scenarios: Executor removes _UUID_CREATE/_UUID_A/_UUID_B/_UUID_C from test_reducer_tag_delta.py per plan instruction, causing NameError on every test in that module.
CI gate (make lint + mypy clean; touched test modules pass) catches it — but only after the mistaken deletion is committed.
findings.suggested_fix: Remove `test_reducer_tag_delta.py` from the Tier-2 dead-symbol list entirely; its `_UUID_*` constants are live.

evidence: tests/scripts/reducer/test_reducer_links.py:471-473: _ALIAS_TARGET_UUID, _ALIAS_LINK_UUID, _ALIAS_SOURCE_UUID defined
tests/scripts/reducer/test_reducer_links.py:499: target_ticket_dir = tracker_dir / _ALIAS_TARGET_UUID

tests/scripts/reducer/test_reducer_links.py:568-569: assert dep['target_id'] == _ALIAS_TARGET_UUID
Plan states 'VERIFIED unreferenced before removal' and 'behavior-preserving'
findings.finding: The plan asserts that `_ALIAS_TARGET_UUID`, `_ALIAS_LINK_UUID`, and `_ALIAS_SOURCE_UUID` in `tests/scripts/reducer/test_reducer_links.py` are dead fixture constants safe to remove. In fact, all three are actively used in test bodies: `_ALIAS_TARGET_UUID` appears in `target_ticket_dir = tracker_dir / _ALIAS_TARGET_UUID` (line 499) and in assertions (lines 548, 568–569); `_ALIAS_SOURCE_UUID` and `_ALIAS_LINK_UUID` are defined alongside and imply the same usage context. Removing them would cause NameError.
findings.location: Tier 2 bullet: 'dead test-fixture constants (_UUID_*/_ALIAS_*/_ENGINE_DIR) in … test_reducer_links'
scenarios: Executor removes _ALIAS_* constants from test_reducer_links.py per plan, causing NameError in test_reducer_link_event_alias_target_normalizes_to_canonical_uuid.
The AC 'each verified unreferenced repo-wide; behavior-preserving' is violated.
findings.suggested_fix: Remove the `_ALIAS_*` entries in `test_reducer_links.py` from the Tier-2 removal list; these constants are live.

evidence: 1 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).
vague/subjective terms present: clean, improve
findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the canonical target signature for `runners.py` `run()` (e.g., 'base signature is `run(self, ctx: Context) -> Result`; override must match exactly') so the executor and reviewer can verify alignment without reading the source.
evidence: 'runners.py (#450): align base/override run() signature' — no target signature stated
AC reads 'runners.py base/override signatures aligned' — 'aligned' is only verifiable if the target is defined; without it the executor must choose an appropriate shape
findings.finding: runners.py scope bullet 'align base/override run() signature' does not specify what the target (correct) signature is, leaving the executor to infer or discover it from the codebase rather than act on a decision already made in the plan.
findings.location: Tier 2 scope bullet: 'runners.py (#450): align base/override run() signature' and corresponding AC 'runners.py base/override signatures aligned'
scenarios: An executor aligns the signatures in a direction that satisfies mypy but mismatches the intended API contract, because the plan never stated which side (base or override) is canonical.
A reviewer cannot evaluate the AC ('signatures aligned') without knowing what the aligned signature is supposed to look like.
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the scope bullet: 'Base `Runner.run(self, ctx: Context) -> Result` is canonical; override `ConcreteRunner.run` must match that signature exactly. Verify with `mypy --strict` on runners.py and its subclasses.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the AI-1 fix and its regression test to also cover `run_differs.py:_read_local_ticket_full` (line 159), which has the identical `_env=None` on the live path and is called with `no_sync=not persist` from the binding-store acting walk.
evidence: Plan states: 'reconcile_helpers.py (AI-1): when no_sync=False, _env=None inherits the parent env'
run_differs.py:159: `_env = dict(_os.environ, REBAR_SYNC_PULL='off') if no_sync else None` — identical pattern in _read_local_ticket_full
reconcile.py:679: `local_reader=lambda lid: _read_local_ticket_full(repo_root, lid, no_sync=not persist)` — same no_sync=False live-path invocation
ABSENCE: run_differs.py is not named anywhere in the AI-1 scope bullet or the AC
findings.finding: The AI-1 fix is scoped only to reconcile_helpers.py._read_local_tickets (line 387), but the identical `_env = dict(_os.environ, REBAR_SYNC_PULL='off') if no_sync else None` pattern exists at run_differs.py:159 in _read_local_ticket_full. Both functions are called with no_sync=not persist from reconcile.py (lines 247 and 679), so the ambient-env inheritance defect is present in both call-sites but the plan only fixes one. The regression test scoped to _read_local_tickets will not cover the _read_local_ticket_full path.
findings.location: Scope / Tier 1 (AI-1) bullet; src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/run_differs.py:159
scenarios: A cap-1 (live sync) reconcile pass runs with REBAR_SYNC_PULL=off in the parent env; _read_local_ticket_full inherits it because its _env=None on the live path, so the binding-store acting walk reads individual tickets without syncing — silent data-staleness on the live path, affecting the hard-delete / reparent branch routing.
findings.suggested_fix: Apply the same explicit-env construction to _read_local_ticket_full: `_env = {k: v for k, v in _os.environ.items() if k != 'REBAR_SYNC_PULL'}` on the live path (no_sync=False), `dict(_os.environ, REBAR_SYNC_PULL='off')` on the no_sync=True path. Add a parallel assertion to the regression test verifying _read_local_ticket_full's subprocess env also strips REBAR_SYNC_PULL on the live path.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for behavior-preservation of Tier-2 removals, e.g., 'run the full unit-test suite (`pytest` or `make test`) — not just touched modules — and confirm it is green before and after removal.'
evidence: 'behavior-preserving' — no test command or suite invocation named to prove this claim
Final AC says 'touched test modules pass' — restricts coverage to files the executor edits, not full regression suite that could catch transitive breakage
findings.finding: The Tier-2 AC 'The 15 Tier-2 symbols removed, each verified unreferenced repo-wide; behavior-preserving' states 'behavior-preserving' but names no concrete proving command; the final meta-AC says only 'touched test modules pass', which may not cover modules whose behavior is transitively preserved by the removed symbols.
findings.location: Acceptance criteria: '- [ ] The 15 Tier-2 symbols removed, each verified unreferenced repo-wide; behavior-preserving.'
scenarios: A symbol believed dead is actually referenced via a dynamic import or string-based lookup not caught by static grep; removing it breaks an untouched module; only 'touched test modules pass' misses the regression.
findings.suggested_fix: Change the AC to: 'The 15 Tier-2 symbols removed; full unit-test suite (`pytest -x`) green after each removal batch; no new mypy errors.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the runners.py signature-alignment direction (which side changes), confirm no external callers are affected or state the migration for those that are, and add a rollback note or expand-contract sequence.
evidence: 'runners.py (#450): align base/override run() signature' — entire description in Scope
AC bullet: 'runners.py base/override signatures aligned' — no direction, no caller impact analysis, no rollback
No mention of whether subclass callers outside runners.py are affected.
findings.finding: The runners.py signature-alignment item states only 'align base/override run() signature' without specifying which direction alignment goes (base adopts override's signature, or vice versa), whether external callers of the overriding class's run() method exist, or providing a rollback/expand-contract path for what is effectively an interface change.
findings.location: Scope / Tier-2 runners.py (#450); AC bullet 5
scenarios: If the fix changes the base class signature to match the override, any other subclasses of the base that override run() with the old signature now have a mismatch that mypy/lint would catch but runtime callers could silently break.
If the fix changes the override to match the base, any call site that passed the override-specific arguments will break at runtime.

findings.finding: The plan asserts _SCAN_MAX_FILES is 'defined + documented but never enforced in search_files', treating it as a dead constant safe to remove. In fact, pai_tools.search_files imports it from fs_tools and enforces it at lines 126-130 (the scan-limit truncation path). Two test files additionally pin it by identity (test_fs_tools_extraction.py:22,41; test_llm_framework.py:186). Removing it from fs_tools.py without updating pai_tools.py and those tests would break the scan-limit feature and the identity-contract tests.
findings.location: Tier 1 scope — llm/fs_tools.py (#291)
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the removal task with: 'Migrate _SCAN_MAX_FILES from fs_tools.py to pai_tools.py as a module-private constant; update the identity-check tests to remove the cross-module assertion; remove the fs_tools definition only after that migration.' Or, keep it in fs_tools.py (where it currently serves as the single source of truth for pai_tools) and remove the finding from the plan entirely.

findings.finding: _SCAN_MAX_FILES is NOT dead in fs_tools.py — it is imported and actively enforced in pai_tools.py (the actual search_files implementation), and two test files assert identity between the two. Removing it would break pai_tools.py and the extraction tests.
findings.location: Tier 1 scope item: 'llm/fs_tools.py (#291): remove the misleading _SCAN_MAX_FILES constant (defined + documented but never enforced in search_files)'
findings.suggested_fix: Relocate _SCAN_MAX_FILES from fs_tools.py to pai_tools.py and update the two test assertions (`assert pai_tools._SCAN_MAX_FILES is fs_tools._SCAN_MAX_FILES` → `assert hasattr(pai_tools, '_SCAN_MAX_FILES')`) and remove the import-sanity line in test_llm_framework.py. Or simply leave the constant in fs_tools.py.

findings.finding: _SCAN_MAX_FILES is actively enforced in pai_tools.search_files (lines 126-129) and pinned by identity in two test files; the plan's characterization that it is 'never enforced' is factually wrong, and removing it would silently break the scan-cap behavior and at least two tests.
findings.location: Scope / Tier 1 — llm/fs_tools.py (#291): 'remove the misleading _SCAN_MAX_FILES constant (defined + documented but never enforced in search_files)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the removal instruction with: 'Verify _SCAN_MAX_FILES is correctly documented in fs_tools.py (it IS enforced via pai_tools import); close #291 as a false positive. No code change needed.'

findings.finding: The plan's core justification for removing `_SCAN_MAX_FILES` — that it is 'defined + documented but never enforced in search_files' — is factually wrong per the codebase. `pai_tools.py` imports it from `fs_tools` (line 32) and actively enforces it inside `search_files` (lines 126–129 of `pai_tools.py`). Two existing tests (`test_fs_tools_extraction.py` lines 22 and 41; `test_llm_framework.py` line 186) assert or import the constant by identity/name. Deleting it as specified would cause a `NameError` in `pai_tools.search_files` at runtime and break the existing test suite — the opposite of the reliability improvement the plan intends.
findings.location: Tier 1 scope bullet: 'llm/fs_tools.py (#291): remove the misleading _SCAN_MAX_FILES constant (defined + documented but never enforced in search_files).' / AC line: '_SCAN_MAX_FILES constant removed; no dangling reference.'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the scope bullet from 'remove the misleading _SCAN_MAX_FILES constant' to 'remove the GitHub finding #291 by adding a comment clarifying that _SCAN_MAX_FILES IS enforced (in pai_tools.search_files, not in fs_tools.search_files directly), or rename it to make the relationship explicit'. If removal is still desired, the plan must also inline the constant's value into pai_tools.py, update the two test identity assertions, and update the llm_framework import sanity line.

findings.finding: The plan instructs removing `_SCAN_MAX_FILES` from `llm/fs_tools.py` on the basis that it is 'defined + documented but never enforced in search_files', but tool-verified evidence shows it IS actively enforced: `pai_tools.py` imports it and enforces it at lines 126-130 of the `search_files` agent tool body (the per-call cap that halts scanning and returns a truncation message). Two test files additionally assert its existence and re-export identity (`test_fs_tools_extraction.py` lines 22/41; `test_llm_framework.py` line 186). Removing the constant destroys the LLM agent's file-scan guardrail without any specified replacement.
findings.location: Tier 1 scope bullet: 'llm/fs_tools.py (#291): remove the misleading _SCAN_MAX_FILES constant'; AC: '_SCAN_MAX_FILES constant removed; no dangling reference.'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the scope bullet to: 'Move `_SCAN_MAX_FILES` from `fs_tools.py` into `pai_tools.py` (where it is consumed and enforced) and update the re-export tests accordingly' — OR remove the bullet if the finding (#291) is based on a now-stale reading of the code.

findings.finding: The plan states _SCAN_MAX_FILES is 'defined + documented but never enforced in search_files,' justifying its removal. In fact the constant is imported from fs_tools into pai_tools and actively enforced at pai_tools.search_files lines 126-129, and is identity-checked in three test locations (test_fs_tools_extraction.py:22, test_fs_tools_extraction.py:41, test_llm_framework.py:186). Deleting it from fs_tools as described — without inlining the value in pai_tools or migrating the import — would break pai_tools at runtime and fail three tests.
findings.location: Scope / Tier 1 bullet: 'llm/fs_tools.py (#291): remove the misleading _SCAN_MAX_FILES constant'
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) move _SCAN_MAX_FILES from fs_tools.py into pai_tools.py as a local constant (since only pai_tools enforces it) and drop the import/identity tests, or (b) keep it in fs_tools but remove the 'misleading' framing from the plan (it IS enforced and IS the correct home if pai_tools consumers rely on it). Update the AC to read 'constant removed from fs_tools and inlined in pai_tools; no dangling import or broken identity test remains.'

findings.finding: The plan lists `_deprecations._KINDS` as a dead module-level constant to remove (Tier-2 cleanup), but it is actively referenced in `tests/unit/test_deprecation_registry.py` line 40 (`dep._KINDS`), making it non-dead; removal would break that test.
findings.location: Scope / Tier 2 — 'Remove genuinely-dead module-level constants/maps in src (… _deprecations _KINDS …)'

findings.finding: The plan lists '_deprecations _KINDS' as a Tier-2 dead constant to remove. The constant _KINDS = frozenset({'env', 'cfg', 'cli', 'lib', 'mcp'}) at _deprecations.py:43 is actively referenced in tests/unit/test_deprecation_registry.py:40 ('assert entry.kind in dep._KINDS'). Removing it would break that test.
findings.location: Tier 2 scope — Remove genuinely-dead module-level constants/maps in src … _deprecations _KINDS
findings.suggested_fix: Remove '_deprecations _KINDS' from the Tier-2 deletion list. If the intent is to inline the check, update test_deprecation_registry.py:40 to compare against the literal set and then remove _KINDS, keeping the behavioral guard intact.

findings.finding: The plan lists `_deprecations _KINDS` as a dead Tier-2 symbol to remove, but test_deprecation_registry.py line 40 references `dep._KINDS` in a live assertion — removing it would break that test.
findings.location: Tier 2 scope item: 'Remove genuinely-dead module-level constants/maps in src (... _deprecations _KINDS ...)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the AI-1 regression test AC to also assert that at least one key inherited from the parent env (e.g. PATH) is present in the subprocess env on the live (no_sync=False) path, proving the dict is derived from os.environ rather than constructed from scratch.
evidence: 'a regression test that asserts the live (no_sync=False) subprocess env has REBAR_SYNC_PULL stripped even when the parent env sets it off, and no_sync=True still sets it off'
No AC clause requiring the test to assert preservation of at least one other parent env var (e.g. PATH) to confirm the dict is derived from os.environ rather than built from scratch.
findings.finding: The AI-1 regression test as specified in the AC only checks REBAR_SYNC_PULL handling. It does not assert that other env vars (e.g. PATH) are still present in the subprocess env on the live path. This means the test would pass even if the fix accidentally constructs a bare minimal env that strips all inherited variables, so the 'behavior-preserving' invariant for the live path is not fully observable.
findings.location: Acceptance criteria — AI-1 regression test AC line
scenarios: Implementation uses a minimal explicit dict; REBAR_SYNC_PULL is absent (correctly), but PATH is also absent; the regression test goes green and the bug ships.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the AC: 'the regression test also asserts that a key present in the ambient env (e.g. PATH) survives into the subprocess env on the live path, confirming env derivation from os.environ.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name production_batch_runner.py as the co-edit target for the runners.py signature alignment, enumerate the call sites in test_production_batch_runner.py, test_project_portability_criterion.py, and test_criteria_overlay.py that pass None, and specify which direction (base relaxed vs override tightened) the alignment takes.
evidence: src/rebar/llm/plan_review/production_batch_runner.py line 69: def run(self, req: BatchRunRequest, agent_runner: AgentStepRunner | None = None)
src/rebar/llm/workflow/runners.py line 94: def run(self, req: BatchRunRequest, agent_runner: AgentStepRunner) — required, non-optional
tests/unit/workflow/test_production_batch_runner.py lines 105, 128, 143, 159, 171, 188, 281, 301, 333, 359, 365: ProductionBatchRunner(runner=fake).run(req, None)
tests/unit/workflow/test_production_batch_runner.py line 144: ProductionBatchRunner(runner=fake).run(req)  — no agent_runner at all
tests/unit/test_project_portability_criterion.py line 182 and tests/unit/test_criteria_overlay.py line 290: .run(req, None)
findings.finding: The runners.py base/override signature alignment (BatchRunner.run requires agent_runner; ProductionBatchRunner.run makes it Optional[AgentStepRunner]=None) has unenumerated consumers that call .run(req, None) or .run(req). The plan names only runners.py as the edit target and does not list production_batch_runner.py or the three test modules that call ProductionBatchRunner.run with None as needing review/update.
findings.location: Tier 2 scope bullet: 'runners.py (#450): align base/override run() signature' and AC: 'runners.py base/override signatures aligned'
scenarios: If alignment means making base BatchRunner.run also accept Optional (matching the override), no callers break but mypy may flag DefaultBatchRunner callers that never pass None
If alignment means making ProductionBatchRunner.run require non-None, all .run(req, None) call sites in 3 test files and gate_dispatch break

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the AI-1 fix description that the new explicit env dict is derived from os.environ (i.e., a copy with REBAR_SYNC_PULL stripped), and acknowledge that other inherited env vars are intentionally preserved.
evidence: 'Build an explicit env dict; set REBAR_SYNC_PULL=off only when no_sync=True; strip the key on the live path.'
No mention of whether other parent-env vars (PATH, HOME, etc.) are preserved in the new explicit dict.
AC regression test only checks REBAR_SYNC_PULL handling, not preservation of other env keys — passes even if interpretation (b) silently breaks callers.
findings.finding: The phrase 'Build an explicit env dict' in the AI-1 fix description is ambiguous: it could mean (a) copy os.environ and strip REBAR_SYNC_PULL, or (b) construct a minimal env from scratch. If interpretation (b) is used, the subprocess would lose PATH, HOME, and every other ambient env var, breaking all callers that rely on them. The plan does not acknowledge callers that depend on the inherited env beyond REBAR_SYNC_PULL, nor does it clarify which interpretation is intended.
findings.location: Scope / Tier 1 — reconcile_helpers.py (AI-1) description
scenarios: A developer implements interpretation (b) — a bare minimal env dict — and the regression test still goes green while every subprocess call now fails due to missing PATH.
Another caller of the fixed function relies on inheriting HOME or PYTHONPATH from the parent env; the new explicit dict silently drops them.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'Build an explicit env dict' with 'Build an explicit env dict starting from a copy of os.environ (e.g., env = {**os.environ}) so that PATH and other inherited vars are preserved; then strip REBAR_SYNC_PULL on the live path and set it to "off" on the no_sync path.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit back-out/rollback note for the AI-1 env-dict behavioral change (e.g., 'revert commit X' or a feature-flag guard) so operators have a documented recovery path if ambient-env callers are affected.
evidence: 'strip the key on the live path' — plan body, Scope AI-1
'Build an explicit env dict' — plan body, Scope AI-1
No rollback/back-out section or expand-contract sequencing mentioned anywhere in the plan for this behavioral change.
findings.finding: The reconcile_helpers.py env-dict change (AI-1) is a runtime behavioral change — callers that currently rely on inheriting an ambient REBAR_SYNC_PULL=off from the parent env on the live path will silently lose that behavior. The plan describes the fix and adds a regression test, but provides no rollback path or back-out plan if the change causes unexpected failures in production or CI environments that set this variable externally.
findings.location: Scope / AI-1 description and AC bullet 1
scenarios: A CI job or deployment script sets REBAR_SYNC_PULL=off in the environment as a global override; after this change, that override is silently ignored on the live path, potentially triggering unexpected live syncs that the operator intended to suppress.
If the regression test passes but an integration environment breaks, there is no stated procedure to revert or expand-contract the change safely.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-line rollback note in the plan: 'Back-out: git revert <commit>; no data migration needed.' Alternatively, guard the new behavior with an explicit env-variable check so callers can opt out during a transition window.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
